completionNotes: Completed as a single-choice radio group pattern with source-backed fieldset/legend guidance, native radio live behavior, select and checkbox distinctions, duplicate audit, and screenshot spot-check evidence.

completionEvidence.patternSpecificGuidance: Guidance names mutually exclusive visible options, fieldset and legend grouping, clickable labels, vertical spacing, cautious defaults, group-level validation, and native keyboard behavior.
completionEvidence.relatedPatternDistinction: The entry separates radio groups from select controls by requiring visible comparison for short important choices, and from checkbox groups by enforcing exactly one selected option.
completionEvidence.liveDemoBehavior: The live demo uses native radio inputs, keeps all options visible, changes one selected plan at a time, validates the group when no option is selected, and shows the selected value.
completionEvidence.comparisonDecisionRules: The linked radio-group-vs-select comparison distinguishes visible short-list comparison from collapsed moderate-list selection and warns against hiding critical choices.
completionEvidence.duplicateAudit: npm run audit:completion compares normalized guidance fields across patterns and currently reports no duplicate field group blocking this completed pattern.
completionEvidence.manualSpotCheck: screenshots/patterns/radio-group.png was inspected after rendering and shows Good UI, Bad UI, Good UX, Bad UX, native radio labels, one-at-a-time selection, and group validation behavior.

name: Radio group
problem: Users need to make one mutually exclusive choice from a short set while seeing every available option before deciding.
solution: Show a labeled group of native radio inputs with visible options, clickable labels, optional hints, one selected value at a time, and group-level validation when required.
problemContext: The option set is short, stable, and mutually exclusive.
Seeing every choice helps users compare plans, answer a question, or avoid missing a consequential option.
selectionRules: Choose radio groups when the user must choose one option from a short visible set.
Make the group question explicit with a legend and keep each option label directly clickable.
Avoid pre-selecting a consequential option unless there is strong evidence that a default is safe and expected.
Use select only when the option list is longer, familiar, or secondary enough to collapse.
requiredStates: No selection state for required unanswered groups.
Group-level validation error when a required choice is missing.
interactionContract: Only one option in the named group can be selected at a time.
The fieldset legend explains the decision being made.
Selection, focus, and required error state remain visible and programmatically exposed.
implementationChecklist: Use native radio inputs where possible before reaching for a custom radiogroup implementation.
Group related options with a fieldset and legend or an equivalent accessible name.
Keep option labels concise, distinct, and large enough to select through the label text.
List options vertically when comparison or touch selection matters.
Show required errors at the group level without clearing a user's existing choice.
commonMisuses: Using radios for independent choices where several answers can be true.
Hiding a short critical set inside a select just to save vertical space.
Omitting the group legend so users hear options without the question.
Pre-selecting a paid, destructive, or biased option without clear evidence.
critiqueQuestions: Is this one mutually exclusive decision with a short enough list to show?
Would hiding these options in a collapsed control make comparison or error recovery worse?
useWhen: Users must choose exactly one option.
There are roughly two to seven meaningful options that benefit from visible comparison.
avoidWhen: The option list is long, searchable, or too low priority to keep expanded.
accessibility: Use a programmatic group label through fieldset/legend or an accessible radiogroup name.
Expose checked, focused, disabled, and required error states.
Associate each label with its radio input so the label is a reliable click target.
keyboardBehavior: Tab enters and leaves the radio group predictably.
Arrow keys move between options in native or APG-compatible radio groups.
Space selects the focused radio option when it is not already selected.
failureModes: Custom buttons without radio semantics or keyboard behavior.
Defaulting to a consequential choice without user intent.
Allowing several options in the group to appear active at once.

sources.note: GOV.UK radios guidance supports one option from a list, fieldset/legend grouping, no careless preselection, and group-level errors.
USWDS radio button guidance supports mutually exclusive choices, label click targets, vertical layout, spacing, and cautious defaults.
APG radio group guidance documents keyboard behavior, roles, checked state, labels, and descriptions for custom implementations.

uiGuidance: Render a fieldset/legend with visible native radio options, selected state, focus state, optional hints, and group-level validation.
Keep short mutually exclusive choices visible instead of collapsing them into a dropdown.
uxGuidance: Help users compare the available options and choose exactly one without opening an extra control.
Avoid biased defaults and do not hide critical short choices inside a collapsed dropdown.

good: Visible native radio options with a clear legend, selected state, focus state, and helper text.
Vertical spacing supports comparison and touch selection without making options feel unrelated.
bad: Custom circles that do not show focus or selected state clearly.
Radio options laid out like unrelated buttons with no group label.

good: Exactly one option is selected at a time and labels are clickable.
Required errors apply to the whole group and do not erase a user's existing selection.
bad: Using radios for choices where multiple selections are allowed.
Defaulting to a consequential choice without user intent.

examples.notes: Documents single-choice radio groups, fieldset and legend grouping, no careless pre-selection, hints, and group-level error messages.

examples.product: U.S. Web Design System Radio buttons component
examples.notes: Documents mutually exclusive radio choices, clickable labels, vertical lists, spacing, cautious defaults, and fieldset/legend accessibility.
Documents expected radio group keyboard interaction, roles, checked state, labels, and described-by behavior for custom implementations.
